title: 中考六选五 8: Why Museums Still Matter
passage:
Today, students can see pictures of almost everything online. They can watch videos of famous paintings, ancient tools and natural wonders. 1. ______ However, museums still have a special value that screens cannot fully replace.

In a museum, objects feel real. A photo of an old bowl may be interesting, but seeing the actual object is different. 2. ______ Students may begin to imagine the hands that made it and the life around it.

Museums also help people slow down. Online information usually comes quickly and disappears quickly. 3. ______ This slower experience can lead to deeper understanding.

Another value of museums is that they connect different subjects. A single exhibition may include history, art, science and geography. 4. ______ This helps students see knowledge as a connected whole instead of separate school subjects.

Of course, museums should not stay old-fashioned. They can use technology to make exhibitions clearer and more interactive. 5. ______ The best museums combine real objects with modern ways of explaining them.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: But technology should support the experience, not replace the real objects.
This may make museums seem less necessary than before.
Standing near it can give students a stronger sense of time and history.
Visitors often need to stop, observe and think carefully.
Students can learn how one idea is linked to another.
Museums are useful only for people who want to become historians.
